# [SN-03] GF16 vs bfloat16 NMSE harness

> Plan ID `SN-03` — local plan identifier in this issue pack, **not** a
> GitHub issue number. See [`00_EPIC_2026.md`](00_EPIC_2026.md).

## Status

🟡 **Protocol committed, harness not yet.** Authoritative protocol is
[`GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md`](../../GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md). The Python
harness `tools/nmse_gf16_bf16.py` is **not yet checked in**. No
`Δ_dB` value is asserted anywhere in the tree.

## Plan

1. Implement `tools/nmse_gf16_bf16.py` matching the schema in
   [`GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md`](../../GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md) §6.
2. Use fp64 reference of the same bit-exact algorithm for `y_ref`;
   never substitute a fused-MAC reference.
3. Input distribution: `N(0, 1)` activations × uniform ternary
   weights from `{−1, 0, +1}`; sample count ≥ 10 000.
4. Pin `git rev-parse HEAD` and the t27 release tag into the output
   JSON.
5. Wire a CI smoke run (small sample count) into
   `.github/workflows/test.yaml`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `tools/nmse_gf16_bf16.py` committed.
- [ ] First run produces a `docs/measurements/nmse_<date>.json`
      matching the schema in §6.
- [ ] CI smoke run passes against the harness.
- [ ] `BENCHMARKS.md` §Demo 1 cross-links the first real measurement
      file.

## R5 honesty

- **No `Δ_dB` value may be published** in `README.md`,
  `BENCHMARKS.md`, or any release note until the harness lands and a
  measurement file exists.
- bf16 reference is computed **host-side in fp64**; there is no
  `src/gf16_to_bf16.v` in this repo and this issue does not propose
  one.
- A favourable `Δ_dB` is **not asserted** by closing this issue —
  only that the harness reproducibly produces a number.

## See also

- [`GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md`](../../GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md) — protocol
- `specs/numeric/gf16.t27`, `specs/numeric/formats.t27`
